steps.title: Spot the contrast between the claims
steps.content: The passage establishes that adornments added substantial weight to Elvis's outfits (25–50 pounds), then introduces a contrasting fact: the fabric was light and flexible. These opposing claims—weight versus ease of movement—signal a contrast.

steps.title: Apply the contrast transition
steps.content: On the other hand marks a contrasting idea—the lightness and flexibility of the fabric directly offset the weight from the adornments, enabling Elvis's freedom of motion. The other options don't signal contrast; they elaborate, exemplify, or lack transitional function. That matches choice A — A is correct.
